quotes.text: I'm not sure that I agree with you a hundred percent on your policework there, Lou.
quotes.speaker: Fargo (1996)
quotes.context: Marge Gunderson (Frances McDormand), the pregnant police chief, gently correcting her deputy Lou's flawed reasoning at a crime scene.

quotes.text: Mr. Vaughn, what we are dealing with here is a perfect engine, an eating machine.
quotes.speaker: Jaws (1975)
quotes.context: Marine biologist Matt Hooper (Richard Dreyfuss) warning Mayor Vaughn (Murray Hamilton) about the shark.

quotes.text: Nobody ever drowned in their own sweat, kid.
quotes.speaker: Rocky (1976)
quotes.context: Mickey, pushing Rocky through a grinding training session
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it sounds exactly like Mickey's gruff, blunt coaching voice, which uses working-class idiom and mild contempt as motivation. The giveaway is it's almost too tidy an aphorism; Mickey's real lines are usually more rambling and specific to the moment.

quotes.text: You fire one of mine, I fire two of yours. That's not revenge, that's arithmetic.
quotes.speaker: The Untouchables (1987)
quotes.context: Eliot Ness, confronting a Capone lieutenant
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the hard-boiled moral arithmetic matches the film's operatic tough-guy register and Ness's crusader voice. The giveaway is the word 'arithmetic,' which is slightly too self-satisfied; the real film's lines lean on blunter Anglo-Saxon vocabulary.

quotes.text: You want to know what I learned in four years? How to wait.
quotes.speaker: The Shawshank Redemption (1994)
quotes.context: Red, talking to a newly arrived inmate in the yard
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the weary wisdom and the yard-conversation context are completely in Red's wheelhouse, and Morgan Freeman's cadence is easy to hear in it. The giveaway is it nearly crosses into the detachable-maxim territory the real film sometimes flirts with but usually earns more organically.

quotes.text: You need to call it. I can't call it for you. It wouldn't be fair.
quotes.speaker: No Country for Old Men (2007)
quotes.context: Anton Chigurh (Javier Bardem) to the gas station proprietor during the coin-toss scene, pressing him to call heads or tails for his life.
